title: CLI lacks the scripts' input shorthands (--writer kind:id, --where field:op:value, CSV --op in)
description: The scripts accept ergonomic shorthands (--writer kind:id parsed to {kind,user}; --where field:op:value split into fields; --op in CSV split to an array); the CLI requires full-JSON or separate-flag forms. Each is a pre-call transform of an already-supported op param.

evidence.reference: GAP-7 in the parity survey, categorized CLI-pre-call

impact: CLI invocations are more verbose and error-prone than the equivalent script invocations for the same ops (e.g. the --writer JSON object vs kind:id) — friction on every authored call.
proposed_resolution: To be determined exactly from live codebase.
